item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha moves off Louisiana, heads for Texas. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: U.S. carries out 12th straight night of strikes as threats to oil trade deepen
item.description: President Trump threatened to bomb an Iranian bridge or power plant for every ship attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, as one shipping insider said "nothing is going through."

item.title: House committee urges DOJ to charge former Trump prosecutor Jack Smith
item.description: The GOP chair of the House Judiciary Committee encouraged the DOJ to look into criminally charging Jack Smith, alleging the former special counsel who brought charges against President Trump lied to Congress.

item.title: Man shot and killed by police in Madison, Wisconsin, sparking protests
item.description: Madison's chief of police says an officer in Wisconsin's capital city shot and killed a man who had injured him with a knife while resisting arrest.

item.title: Man embraces remote work by setting up offices at the beach, baseball game
item.description: A California man is taking advantage of remote work with viral office settings that are attracting fans, critics, and thousands of views. Gwen Baumgarder joined him for a workday at the ballpark.

item.title: Comforting news for coffee drinkers concerned about their heart health
item.description: When it comes to heart health.. how many cups of caffeinated coffee are safe to drink each day? A new statement from the American Heart Association may be reassuring to coffee drinkers. Gwen Baumgardner has more.

item.title: Amish Shah projected to win Arizona's 1st District Democratic primary
item.description: Democrat Amish Shah will be up against Republican Jay Feely in Arizona's 1st Congressional District in November.

item.title: National Guard deployed as flash floods hit West Virginia after 7 inches of rain
item.description: Torrential rains in West Virginia have caused flash flooding that inundated homes and businesses this week.

item.title: Mamdani says he won't try to arrest Israel's Netanyahu
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani conceded late Tuesday that the city does not have the authority to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: ICE detentions hit highest monthly total in June since crackdown began
item.description: More than 43,000 immigrants were arrested last month as DHS continues to detain people in less-publicized enforcement surges.

item.title: Climate change to obliterate $1.5 trillion in U.S. home values
item.description: More than 5 million Americans will leave areas being impacted by climate change this year, according to study.

item.title: Pastor sues OpenAI alleging ChatGPT's medical advice nearly killed him
item.description: The case accuses OpenAI of practicing medicine without a license and failing to safeguard users seeking health advice.

item.title: Trump attends dignified transfer of soldiers killed in Iranian attacks
item.description: President Trump attended a dignified transfer honoring the four U.S. Army soldiers killed in the Middle East in recent days during the ongoing war with Iran.

item.title: CBS News crew captured during Tiananmen Square massacre (1989)
item.description: Longtime CBS News photographer Derek Williams has died. In June 1989, Williams and CBS News correspondent Richard Roth were detained by Chinese authorities during the Tiananmen Square massacre. They would eventually be released, relatively unharmed. These were the reports they filed through the chaos.

item.title: Florida police spray bullets at homicide suspect's vehicle following car chase
item.description: Florida police fired a barrage of bullets at a vehicle belonging to a man wanted in the killing of a mother of four after he led them on a wild car chase. The suspect was taken into custody. Cristian Benavides has more.

item.title: Salazar responds to Sheinbaum's claim that he lied about "El Mayo" capture: "Truth is the truth"
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has accused Ken Salazar, who served as both a senator and the U.S. ambassador to Mexico, of lying about the 2024 capture of Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada. Salazar, who writes about the issue in his new book, "Borderlands," joins "The Takeout" with his response.

item.title: Raffensperger reacts to improper noncitizen voter registrations in New Jersey, DHS claims
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill said on Tuesday that, three years ago, a computer glitch resulted in some 6,600 noncitizens being registered to vote in the state. Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger joins to discuss.

item.title: Secret Service warns of high threat levels before White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: Secret Service officials warned nationwide threats have reached an unprecedented level since January, nearly 40% higher than during the same period last year.

item.title: 7/22: CBS Evening News
item.description: Florida police spray bullets at homicide suspect's vehicle following car chase; rocket launcher hoax shakes LaGuardia Airport.

item.title: Council handpicked by Trump to gut review process of historic-preservation projects
item.description: The Advisory Council for Historic Preservation, whose members were mostly handpicked by President Trump, is poised to end mandatory state review and public comment on projects affecting historical properties.

item.title: House GOP advances $95 billion plan to fund Iran war and SAVE America Act
item.description: House Republicans adopted a budget resolution that kickstarts the party-line reconciliation process. It now heads to the Senate, where it'll face an uphill battle.

item.title: 7/22: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump and Iran trade threats to attack energy infrastructure; Trump administration officially announces nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia.

item.title: House passes bill to limit stock purchases by members of Congress
item.description: The Stop Insider Trading Act would prohibit members of Congress, their spouses and dependent children from purchasing publicly traded stocks.

item.title: What Trump's generic drug tariffs could mean, according to experts
item.description: Health policy experts say higher import duties could increase costs and strain supplies while offering no guarantee manufacturers will shift production to the U.S.

item.title: House and Senate take different paths to avoid shutdown before midterms
item.description: Another deadline to fund the government is looming this fall in Congress, and lawmakers seem eager to avoid another costly impasse ahead of the midterms.

item.title: "Nothing is going through" the Strait of Hormuz, shipping insider says
item.description: Despite U.S. assertions, the CEO of a maritime risk firm says "there is no guarantee of safe passage" through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Nolan Wells' cause of death undetermined, family's autopsy shows
item.description: Nolan Wells, 18, was found dead just off Horn Island in Mississippi after enjoying a day out with friends on July 4.

item.title: DOJ sues over immersion water heaters that can burst into flames
item.description: The Justice Department is trying to force the two Chinese companies to recall immersion water heaters used for bathtubs and pools.

item.title: Lung cancer in nonsmokers: "A women's health crisis"
item.description: Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer death in American women — and a growing share of new cases are in women who've never smoked.

item.title: Tenisha Warner, widow of Malcolm-Jamal Warner, opens up about being a single mom
item.description: Tenisha Warner sat down for an exclusive interview with "CBS Mornings" before a lawsuit over her late husband's estate became public, and discussed her grief and raising her daughter alone.
